title: Playtest case file
description: One test case (<name>.yaml): a journey (regression with a deterministic gate) or a discovery study (open-ended exploration, no gate). Any key left unset inherits from the nearest playtest.yaml up the directory chain (nearest wins). max_steps/timeout may also be spelled nested under limits.

story.description: Required. The user's goal in natural language, addressed to the actor in the second person ("You keep forgetting to buy milk..."). Journey stories name concrete targets in double quotes; discovery stories state a goal without prescribing the path ("Get your timesheet data out of the platform however seems natural").

description.description: Optional one-line human-facing summary, e.g. "Add a todo and see it in the list". Shown in run lists (the viewer's picker) instead of the story prose; never sent to the actor, so it cannot change agent behavior. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml.

tags.description: Free-form labels for --tag filtering, e.g. [smoke]. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml. Default [].

persona.description: Persona the actor role-plays: built-in "tester" or "exploratory", or the name/slug of a personas/*.yaml file. A scalar runs one actor (default "tester", inheritable). A list (e.g. [first-timer, power-user]) fans out one run per persona in discovery — id <case-id>@<persona>, the persona overridden per run; on a journey only the first is used (a journey has one recorded path) and the rest are ignored with a warning.

mode.description: Case kind, default "journey". journey: regression test — deterministic success gate, baseline record/act/heal, pass/fail exit codes. discovery: product-insight study — no success gate, always a fresh agentic run that ends "explored" (give_up is a valid, informative outcome), graded with the discoverability rubric. Inheritable: a study directory usually sets mode: discovery once in its playtest.yaml.

vision.description: Discovery capability: send each step's viewport screenshot to the actor alongside the a11y snapshot and ask for visual observations (the step's "visual" field). Defaults to true when the resolved mode is discovery, false otherwise. Inheritable. "vision: true" resolving on a journey case is a config error — the regression instrument stays a11y-only by construction; "vision: false" is always allowed.

visual_regression.description: Internal: enable the pixel drift channel — hash each step's screenshot and compare on replay (a divergence escalates to heal, like a11y drift). No images are sent to the model. web only. Default true. Inheritable.

visual_regression_drift.description: Internal tuning knob for visual_regression: the Hamming-distance threshold (0–64) over the per-step screenshot dHash above which the screenshot reads as pixel drift. Higher = more tolerant (fewer drift heals); lower = stricter. Default 10.

label.description: Optional short human name for this criterion (e.g. "Single parent is not eligible"), useful when an assert claim is a long sentence. Purely cosmetic: shown in the burned clip's summary card (and run lists), never sent to the grader and never affecting the pass/fail verdict. The entry still carries exactly one check kind alongside label.

url_matches.description: Glob (* and ?) matched against the final URL or its pathname, e.g. "/done/*".

element_exists.description: web only: CSS selector that must match on the final page, e.g. "[data-testid=todo-item]".

screen_shows.description: mobile only: an accessibility id / predicate that must resolve on the final screen — the mobile analog of element_exists, e.g. "~todo-cell-buy-milk".

response_status.description: api only: the status of the last response, exact ("201") or a class ("2xx").

response_matches.description: api only: a JSON-path/value over the last response body, deterministic (no model), e.g. "$.title == \"buy milk\"" or "completed == false". Natural-language response claims use assert instead.

api_called.description: "METHOD /path/glob" that must appear in the run's network traffic, e.g. "POST /api/todos" or "PATCH /api/todos/*". web and api only; mobile has no network capture in v1, so api_called on a mobile case is a config error.

assert.description: Natural-language claim about the final state — the page (web), the final screen (mobile), or the last response body (api) — checked by the grader model. Costs one model call per run, even on acted runs.

console_errors.description: web only: maximum console error count over the whole run, e.g. 0. Deterministic (no model call) — a correctness gate, not a perf budget.

accessibility_violations.description: web only: maximum number of axe-core (WCAG 2.0 A/AA + 2.1 AA) violation nodes summed across the run, e.g. 0. Counts EVERY violation on each step's page (a full-page axe run per step), not just those on the element the agent touched. Deterministic (no model call); a HARD compliance gate. The grade.json a11y summary reports the same total plus a per-rule breakdown for the report.

success.description: Journey-only deterministic gate: every criterion must pass, each entry exactly one of url_matches/element_exists/screen_shows/api_called/response_status/response_matches/console_errors/accessibility_violations/assert (optionally plus a cosmetic "label"). The "exactly one kind" half is enforced in config.js, not by this schema — once a label may sit alongside the kind, the schema alone can only bound the entry to at most two keys. Each kind is valid only for its driver(s) (web: url_matches/element_exists/api_called/console_errors/accessibility_violations/assert; mobile: screen_shows/assert; api: url_matches/api_called/response_status/response_matches/assert) — a wrong-driver kind is a config error. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml. Config error in a discovery case (ask report: questions instead). Default []. The success-item properties listed here are the BUILT-IN kinds; a suite's custom assertions (an assertions/<name>/assertion.js under the suite root) extend them at load time — each assertion-owned key is injected (as a string-valued property) into a per-suite clone of this schema before validation, so the key is usable in success: exactly like a built-in. additionalProperties stays false (a typo'd key is a named config error, never a silent no-op); this static schema ships NO assertion keys. See docs/CONTRACTS.md §7 and docs/ASSERTIONS.md.

lcp_ms.description: Threshold on the WORST navigation LCP in milliseconds: "< 2500" style (ops < ≤ > ≥) or a bare number meaning ≤ n.

input_to_paint_ms.description: Threshold on the WORST input-to-paint latency in milliseconds; same format as lcp_ms.

perf.description: Latency thresholds (LCP / input-to-paint) checked with the journey gate. Inheritable. Silently irrelevant in discovery (the gate never runs). No-console-errors is a success criterion, not a perf key.

report.description: Report questions the grader answers from the trajectory, e.g. "At which screen would this user have expected an export affordance?". Questions, not assertions — findings have answers, not pass/fail. Most useful in discovery studies, but honored on journey cases too. Case-only: not allowed in playtest.yaml. Default [].

max_steps.description: Maximum actor steps before the run ends with end_reason max_steps. Mode-aware default when unset: 50 for a journey, 300 for a discovery run (which explores freely). Inheritable. Equivalent nested spelling: limits.max_steps.

timeout.description: Wall-clock limit per run: "5m", "90s", "250ms", or a number of milliseconds (must be > 0). Mode-aware default when unset: "4m" for a journey, "30m" for a discovery run. Inheritable. Equivalent nested spelling: limits.timeout.

limits.description: Nested spelling of max_steps/timeout; normalized to the top-level keys at load time.

actor_model.description: Model that role-plays the user. A short tier enum — "sonnet", "haiku", "opus", "gpt5_4_mini" (default), "gpt5_4", "gpt5_5" — resolves to a fully-qualified gateway model name (override per enum with PLAYTEST_<ENUM>_MODEL, e.g. PLAYTEST_SONNET_MODEL); any other value is used verbatim as an already-qualified name. A capable actor reads the page and recovers from friction far more reliably than a cheap one, and for both journeys and discovery the trajectory is only worth grading if the user was played well; pin a stronger tier ("sonnet"/"opus") when fidelity matters more than cost. Inheritable.

grader_model.description: Model that grades finished runs and checks assert: criteria. A short tier enum — "sonnet", "haiku", "opus", "gpt5_4_mini", "gpt5_4", or "gpt5_5" (default) — resolves to a fully-qualified gateway model name (override with PLAYTEST_<ENUM>_MODEL); any other value is used verbatim. Inheritable.

driver.description: Transport for the app under test. Default "web" (Playwright/Chromium). "mobile" drives a native iOS/Android app via Appium; "api" drives a REST API via fetch. Inheritable — usually set once in a playtest.yaml. The other app.* keys, success kinds, and perf keys are validated per-driver.

base_url.description: Where the app under test runs, e.g. http://localhost:4173. Required for web and api (or pass --base-url); optional for mobile. Agents really click buy/delete/submit — point at staging or test, never production.

compose.description: docker-compose file path, relative to this file: managed mode — the harness boots and tears down the app per run. web/api.

init.description: Executable script path, relative to this file, run before each case to reset app state. Receives BASE_URL/RUN_ID. Any driver — also the mobile/api pre-auth path.

storage_state.description: Playwright storage-state JSON path, relative to this file, for a pre-authenticated browser session. web only.

platform.description: mobile only: the target platform (Appium XCUITest for ios, UiAutomator2 for android).

app.description: mobile only: path (relative to this file) to the app binary to install — a .app/.ipa (ios) or .apk (android). Required for the mobile driver.

device.description: mobile only: the target device/emulator/simulator name or udid. Omit to let the driver pick a default available device.

appium_url.description: mobile only: URL of a running Appium server, e.g. http://localhost:4723. Omit to use the default local Appium endpoint.

preserve_session.description: mobile only: keep the app installed and its data intact across runs instead of the default reinstall-and-wipe (maps to Appium's noReset). Off by default — every session reinstalls the binary and clears state, so the actor starts from a clean install each run. Set true when reinstalling is costly or would drop a login you've already established (a signed-in build): the app is left installed with its data, so the actor stays authenticated between runs. The app binary is still required and is installed on first launch.

openapi.description: api only: path (relative to this file) to an OpenAPI spec. Its operations become the interactable "elements" the actor sees; recommended for journey (baseline-grade) api cases. Without it, the actor infers paths from the story (exploratory only).

width.description: Browser viewport width in CSS pixels. Default 1280.

height.description: Browser viewport height in CSS pixels. Default 720. A number captures VIEWPORT-ONLY stills (exactly what the user saw on screen — content below the fold is cut off, faithful to perception). Set null to capture FULL-PAGE stills (the whole scrollable page; good for debugging scroll or marketing shots). Whichever is chosen drives BOTH the on-disk PNG and the vision image sent to the actor.

viewport.description: web only: the Chromium viewport in CSS pixels, e.g. { width: 1366, height: 768 } for a smaller desktop. Default { width: 1280, height: 720 }. Shapes the layout the app renders at; the height also decides whether stills are viewport-only (a number) or full-page (null). Riding manifest.pins.viewport, this is part of the comparability key — runs with a different viewport (including viewport-only vs full-page) are not compared.

device_scale_factor.description: web only: the Chromium device scale factor (DPI multiplier) for step screenshots. Default 1; set 2 for retina-crisp, higher-resolution stills (the on-disk PNG renders at 2x the pixel density). Purely a rendering-quality knob — it does NOT ride manifest.pins, so it is not part of the comparability key and a run at a different scale factor still compares against the baseline. The vision image sent to the actor is still capped at the longest-edge limit, so raising this sharpens the disk artifact without changing token cost.

dom_quiet_ms.description: web only: how long the DOM must go without mutations before the page counts as settled. Default 500.

net_quiet_ms.description: web only: how long there must be no in-flight network requests before the page counts as settled. Default 500.

source_quiet_ms.description: mobile only: how long the accessibility tree must go unchanged before the screen counts as settled. Default 400.

max_ms.description: Hard ceiling on the settle wait — a screen that never goes quiet (e.g. a poller) stops waiting here. Default 10000.

initial_quiet_ms.description: web + mobile: the quiet window to require on the FIRST settle only (initial page/screen load), capped at max_ms. Default max(2× the per-step quiet window, 500). Raise it when heavy parallel load makes the initial render lag so the actor never snapshots a half-loaded/blank first page; subsequent steps keep the fast per-step windows. Rides manifest.pins.settle only when set.

settle.description: web + mobile: override the driver's settle quiet windows. After every action the driver waits until things go quiet (web: DOM AND network for dom_quiet_ms/net_quiet_ms; mobile: the accessibility tree for source_quiet_ms) — capped at max_ms — before snapshotting, so the actor always sees a stable page. Lower the windows for a fast app to shave the per-step floor; raise them for an async-heavy one. The FIRST settle of a session (initial load) waits longer — initial_quiet_ms, default max(2× the per-step window, 500ms) — so a lagging app doesn't settle on a blank first page. Use the keys for your driver (web: dom_quiet_ms/net_quiet_ms; mobile: source_quiet_ms; both: max_ms/initial_quiet_ms). Riding manifest.pins.settle, this is part of the comparability key — a tuned value means runs with a different value are not compared.

cookies.description: web only: cookies to set on the browser context before the first navigation, as a flat { name: value } map, e.g. { bvt: "true" }. Scoped domain-wide (base_url's host, path "/") so they ride EVERY request the app makes — useful for routing to a blue/green slot or a feature flag. A session input (like storage_state), not a manifest pin: a run with different cookies still compares against the baseline.

base_url.description: This environment's URL, e.g. https://stg.example.com. The main thing that varies per env.

cookies.description: Per-env cookies ({ name: value }) — e.g. a blue/green-slot or feature-flag cookie that only staging needs. Replaces the top-level app.cookies wholesale (no merge).

storage_state.description: Path (relative to this file) to a Playwright storage-state JSON — a saved cookies + localStorage snapshot of an already-logged-in browser session, so the actor starts authenticated instead of signing in. Per-env because each environment usually has its own accounts/tokens (a dev login won't authenticate against staging). web only.

init.description: Path (relative to this file) to a per-env state-reset/seed script run before each case (receives BASE_URL/RUN_ID) — use when an environment needs a different seed than the default.

envs.description: Named environment overlays selected with --env <name>. Each <name> is a partial app.* block (base_url/cookies/storage_state/init) whose keys SHALLOW-override the top-level app.* (an env inherits the defaults, then replaces per key — e.g. envs.stg.cookies replaces the top-level cookies wholesale, no deep merge). Use for the same story across dev/staging/prod. Usually set on the suite's playtest.yaml. With no --env the top-level app.* runs; --base-url still wins over --env. Transport-level keys (driver/compose/mobile/api config) are deliberately NOT per-env, and envs cannot nest.

app.description: App under test. Merges per-key down the defaults chain, so a case can override just base_url. The app.driver key selects which subset applies (see its enum); a key set for the wrong driver is a config error. app.envs declares named environment overlays selected at run time with --env <name>.
